defects.claim: After interviewing roughly 30,000 people, Oprah arrived at a single unifying finding: every guest was silently asking the same three-part question — Was that okay?
defects.corpus_value: thousands of interviews (not ~30,000), and a two-part question (did you hear me / did what I say matter), not three-part
defects.evidence_quote: What I learned in all of those thousands of interviews is that there is a common denominator in all of our human experience. Everybody wants to know, did you hear me, and did what I say matter?
defects.why: The corpus states 'thousands of interviews' and a two-part question ('did you hear me' and 'did what I say matter'), not 30,000 interviews and a three-part question as claimed.

defects.claim: This is why she ended the show at its peak in 2011 and stepped down from the Weight Watchers board in 2024.
defects.why: The corpus contains no mention of the show ending in 2011 or stepping down from the Weight Watchers board in 2024; these hard figures and events are entirely absent from the corpus.
